title: Statement Regarding MF Global Inc.

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York suspended MF Global Inc. from conducting new business with the New York Fed on October 31, 2011. The suspension remains in effect until MF Global establishes it can satisfy the NY Fed's primary dealer policy requirements or until the NY Fed terminates MF Global's primary dealer status.
key_points: MF Global Inc. was suspended from conducting new business with the Federal Reserve Bank of New York on October 31, 2011.
The suspension continues until MF Global demonstrates it can satisfy requirements under the NY Fed's 'Administration of Relationships with Primary Dealers' policy.
The NY Fed reserves the right to terminate MF Global's status as a primary dealer entirely.
The NY Fed's primary dealer policies are published online at http://www.newyorkfed.org/markets/primarydealers.html.
why_it_matters: This suspension from primary dealer operations affected MF Global's ability to interact with the Federal Reserve in open market operations and Treasury trading. Primary dealers are essential intermediaries in Treasury markets, and suspension or removal from this status is a severe market integrity finding. MF Global subsequently filed for bankruptcy on October 31, 2011.
market_structure_context: This statement concerns the primary dealer framework administered by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York. Primary dealers serve as counterparties for Federal Reserve open market operations and play a critical role in Treasury market liquidity and price discovery. The suspension of a primary dealer represents a significant disruption to market plumbing and dealer network integrity.

human_review.notes: Source is a brief official statement. Content is factual and directly supported by the text. Historical record noted for age.

notes: Historical record from October 31, 2011. While relevant to market structure (primary dealer system), the age significantly limits utility for current analysis. MF Global's subsequent bankruptcy makes this statement a historical marker of that crisis. Source is thin in content but factually reliable as an official Fed statement.
